title: Brown Gravy

instruction.title: 1. Caramelize the Onions
steps: Heat oil in a heavy-bottom pan.
Add bay leaves, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, cumin, mace, and peppercorns.
Add sliced onions and cook over medium heat for 30–40 minutes until deep golden brown.
Avoid burning the onions to prevent bitterness.

instruction.title: 2. Add Aromatics
steps: Add ginger, garlic, and green chilies.
Cook for 2–3 minutes until the raw aroma disappears.

instruction.title: 3. Cook the Tomatoes
steps: Add tomatoes, salt, turmeric, coriander powder, and chili powder.
Continue until oil begins separating from the masala.

instruction.title: 4. Simmer
steps: Pour in hot water or stock.
Reduce heat and simmer gently for 30 minutes.

instruction.title: 5. Blend Smooth
steps: Blend the gravy until completely smooth.
Cool slightly before using a standard blender.

chefTips: Refrigerate for up to 5 days or freeze for 2–3 months.
Restaurants often use extra oil for better storage and reheating.
For korma, add cream and nuts.
For rogan josh, add yogurt and extra red chili.
For bhuna, cook meat directly in the gravy until thick.
For richer color and flavor, add 1 tbsp roasted gram flour (besan) while frying onions.
